text.title: Weaviate — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 rules: v4 client pinned, schema-first design, native hybrid not client fusion, replication factor at schema creation, multi-vector for multi-modal.

rule.statement: Python client MUST be pinned to v4 or later. Mixing v3 + v4 in the same codebase is forbidden.
rule.rationale: v4 broke compatibility with v3; mixing produces silent runtime errors in connection handling.

rule.statement: Classes, properties, and cross-references MUST be defined in schema before upsert. Schema-on-write is forbidden; runtime field addition risks tokenizer drift.
rule.rationale: Implicit schema changes silently re-index parts of the corpus and corrupt sparse-index stats used for BM25.

rule.statement: Hybrid scoring (vector + BM25) MUST use the native `Hybrid(query, alpha)` query, NOT client-side fusion of separate vector + keyword calls.
rule.rationale: Native fusion uses corpus-aware BM25 stats; client-side fusion misweights rare terms and inflates latency by 2x.

rule.statement: Replication factor + sharding config MUST be set at schema/class creation. Changing them later requires class recreation + re-import.
rule.rationale: Topology is bound at class creation; runtime change requires migration.

rule.statement: Multi-modal data (text + image) MUST use named vectors per modality on one object, NOT separate classes per modality.
rule.rationale: Separate classes break joint ranking and cross-reference queries.
